topic: I have dreamed of the same house my entire life. I found it on Google Street View last week and it is real.
title: I Have Dreamed of the Same House My Entire Life. I Found It on Google Street View | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: I have dreamed of the same house my entire life. Last week I found it on Street View.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing Google Street View of a two-story white house with a red mailbox, a crack visible in the front step, the image slightly blurred from Street View compression, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: White. Two stories. A red mailbox. A crack in the front step shaped like a river.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a child's pencil drawing on aged paper showing a simple two-story house with a mailbox and a jagged crack drawn in the front step, the paper yellowed and creased, natural light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I have drawn it since I was a child. Always the same house. Always the same details.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a desk covered in drawings of varying ages, all showing the same house from the same angle, some in crayon, some in pencil, some clearly adult handwriting, desk lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I reverse-image searched one of my drawings. A real estate listing came up. Sold in 2003.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a real estate listing with a photo of a white two-story house matching the drawings exactly, the sale date visible, dim room light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I found it on Street View. Every detail correct. The crack. The mailbox. The broken shutter.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop showing Street View side by side with one of the pencil drawings on the desk, the two images nearly identical, a finger pointing at the matching crack in the step, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I reached out to the current owner through the listing agency. She replied immediately.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing an email inbox, a new reply notification open, the sender name visible but the message text just out of frame, screen glow in a dark room, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: She said she knew someone would come looking eventually.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing the opened email, the first line reading she knew someone would come eventually, the rest of the message below the fold, dark room, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The previous owner left something for me. She used my full name. I had not given it.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of the email screen scrolled down showing a photo attachment of a sealed envelope with a name handwritten on the front, dim room, screen glow only light source, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The handwriting on the envelope was mine. The postmark read 1987. I was born in 1991.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a physical envelope on a table, a postmark stamp visible in the corner with a year clearly readable, the name on the front in familiar handwriting, kitchen light above, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Inside was a single index card. A drawing of the house. Identical to mine.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a small index card on a table, a simple pencil sketch of a two-story house with a mailbox and a cracked step, next to one of the narrator's own drawings for comparison, both nearly identical, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: On the back, one sentence. You will find this when you are ready to come back.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close up of the back of the index card, a single line of handwriting reading YOU WILL FIND THIS WHEN YOU ARE READY TO COME BACK, the ink slightly faded, natural light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I have a new dream now. I am at the front door. It is open. Something inside is waiting.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of the white two-story house at dusk, the front door open and dark inside, the red mailbox at the path, the cracked front step visible, no lights on inside, no people, vertical 9:16 framing
